success_factors: Workflow status is success with all planned nodes executed end-to-end (download, analysis, env, generate, run, review, finalize).
Import-first adapter strategy was feasible (import_feasibility 0.82) and MCP plugin started healthy over stdio.
Repository ingestion succeeded via zip fallback despite DeepWiki failure, allowing static AST-based discovery and generation.
No runtime errors or warnings were recorded in the workflow.
failure_reasons: Quality-gap failures: original project tests not executed/passed (reported passed=false with empty details) and MCP test evidence is minimal.
DeepWiki analysis failed, reducing external contextual enrichment.
Generated MCP surface appears over-broad and potentially noisy (very large endpoint list, collisions like repeated params/softmax).

node_performance.download_time: Completed successfully; repository imported via zip fallback. No per-node timing provided, but download was not a blocker.
node_performance.analysis_time: Likely dominant phase due to AST scan across ~997 files and large symbol extraction; completed without errors.
node_performance.generation_time: Completed and produced 7 MCP artifacts; generation appears fast relative to deep analysis, but exact timing unavailable.
node_performance.test_time: Plugin health check passed, but substantive test execution is effectively absent (original tests empty), so validation depth is low.
